other_observers: Brenda Linfield several other birders were there at the same time as us, but I do not know their names; I was not the original finder of this bird. Its presence had previously been reported on cobirds.
description: The bird was generally the size and shape of a Western Tanager, but perhaps a bit plumper.  The bill was thick, dull orange in color.  The cap, nape, and mantle were a uniform, unstreaked greenish color.  The face and throat were unstreaked dull yellow, with a very narrow white eye ring.  The belly was whitish, and the undertail coverts were pale yellow.  The wings were dark and unmarked (no wingbars), with a large, uniform black patch on the shoulders, and dark gray below the patch.Behaviors: It would perch in a tree or on a roof for a few minutes, then fly to a different tree or building. It had a roughly horizontal posture - not the vertical posture of a flycatcher.Call: none heardPlumage: adult nonbreeding
